methods.description: Fieldwork conducted over several summers to collect stratigraphy descriptions, charcoal samples for radiocarbon dating, and sediment samples for various analyses.

steps.description: Conducted fieldwork in the summers of 2007, 2008, 2010, 2011, and 2016.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct fieldwork to collect samples and data

steps.description: In summer 2011, described stratigraphy of 50 terrace scarps and collected charcoal samples for radiocarbon dating and sediment samples for grain-size analysis, thin sections, and optically stimulated luminescence (OSL) dating.
steps.searchable_summary: collect stratigraphy descriptions and samples for analysis

methods.description: Petrographic analysis to characterize mineralogy of loess samples using thin sections and point counting.

steps.description: Embedded sediment from samples in epoxy, made thin sections, and analyzed using a petrographic microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare and analyze thin sections of sediment samples

steps.description: Point counted at least 475 grains from each sample to determine percentages of quartz, calcite, and mica.
steps.searchable_summary: perform point counting to quantify mineral composition

methods.description: Dating methods to determine the timing of loess deposition using radiocarbon and luminescence techniques.

steps.description: Charcoal, grain, wood, or bone samples for radiocarbon dating were pretreated with weak HCl, carbon dioxide was extracted and graphitized on an iron catalyst.
Used AMS to measure the graphitized carbon dioxide for 14C/12C ratios.
steps.searchable_summary: measure carbon isotope ratios using AMS

steps.description: Calibrated radiocarbon ages to calendar years with CALIB v. 8.2 using the IntCal20 data set.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate radiocarbon ages to calendar years

steps.description: Dated sherds using thermoluminescence, infrared stimulated luminescence, and OSL.

methods.description: Analysis of grain-size distributions of loess and gravelly loess using laser diffractometry and QGrain software.

steps.description: Pretreated sediment with hydrogen peroxide to remove organics.
Used a Mastersizer laser diffractometer to quantify the grain-size distribution of the <2 mm fraction of samples.
steps.searchable_summary: measure grain-size distribution using laser diffractometry

steps.description: Used QGrain software to separate grain-size distributions of mixed samples into component parts.
